channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu at odds after heated call over Israel's offensive into Lebanon
item.description: President Trump admits to calling Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "crazy" during a tense and heated phone call over Israel's offensive into Lebanon. How strained is their relationship?
item.content:encoded: President Trump admits to calling Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "crazy" during a tense and heated phone call over Israel's offensive into Lebanon. How strained is their relationship?
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to a ceasefire. Could it affect U.S.-Iran negotiations?
item.description: President Trump is demanding that Israel's prime minister end the incursion into Lebanon. Danny Citrinowicz of the Institute for National Security Studies shares his view.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is demanding that Israel's prime minister end the incursion into Lebanon. Danny Citrinowicz of the Institute for National Security Studies shares his view.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Rubio says white South Africans 'assimilate' easier when questioned about program
item.description: The Trump Administration stopped admitting refugees and created a new program for white South Africans. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers this week that they "assimilate" more easily.
item.content:encoded: The Trump Administration stopped admitting refugees and created a new program for white South Africans. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers this week that they "assimilate" more easily.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Some Republicans' growing frustrations with Trump is beginning to show, Trump and Netanyahu have a tense call over Israel's actions in Lebanon, Russia's economic forum opens.
item.content:encoded: Some Republicans' growing frustrations with Trump is beginning to show, Trump and Netanyahu have a tense call over Israel's actions in Lebanon, Russia's economic forum opens.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Sting talks about the comeback tour of his musical, 'The Last Ship'
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Sting about the comeback tour of his musical "The Last Ship," which has a run at the Metropolitan Opera next week.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Sting about the comeback tour of his musical "The Last Ship," which has a run at the Metropolitan Opera next week.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Leon Panetta on Trump's controversial pick for acting national intelligence director
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Defense Secretary and CIA Director Leon Panetta about President Trump's controversial appointment of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Defense Secretary and CIA Director Leon Panetta about President Trump's controversial appointment of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center will open soon. Here's a sneak peek
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month. NPR got a preview.
item.content:encoded: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month. NPR got a preview.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Critics of Trump's proposed arch say it would obstruct symbolic vista
item.description: President Trump's proposed arch, planned to sit between the Lincoln Memorial and the home of Confederate Gen. Robert E. Lee, would block the symbolic view between the two and the message of unity it represents.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's proposed arch, planned to sit between the Lincoln Memorial and the home of Confederate Gen. Robert E. Lee, would block the symbolic view between the two and the message of unity it represents.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Pause on visa processing leaves Iranian nationals anxious
item.description: The U.S. has long attracted top talent to come study and work. Many are now at risk of being deported because of a pause on visa processing. For Iranian nationals, returning home is risky.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. has long attracted top talent to come study and work. Many are now at risk of being deported because of a pause on visa processing. For Iranian nationals, returning home is risky.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: With World Cup set to begin, public health officials hope to defend against germs
item.description: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!
item.content:encoded: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang
